message:
🌱 wasmedge-runtime: bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file. (#1880)

* 🌱 Add wasmedge-runtime: bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is mission

* fix: replace closed WasmEdge PR #3623 with merged PR #4261

The original fix PR WasmEdge/WasmEdge#3623 was closed without merging.
Replace with WasmEdge/WasmEdge#4261 which was merged and actually
fixes the uninstaller issue.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: replace closed WasmEdge PR #3623 with merged PR #4261

The original fix PR WasmEdge/WasmEdge#3623 was closed without merging.
Replace with WasmEdge/WasmEdge#4261 which was merged and actually
fixes the uninstaller issue.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

However, it will run the uninstaller first to remove the previous installation. Then, it encountered the bug."

+        "title": "Apply the fix for bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is…",
+        "description": "It's on macOS. Additionally, according to the report, it will occur on both Intel and Apple Silicon platforms. The root cause of this issue is that the command usage of `sed` differs between macOS and Linux. See https://github.com/WasmEdge/WasmEdge/issues/3185#issuecomment-2187919503\n\nTherefore, you just need to create a .bash_profile on macOS and try running the installer and uninstaller to trigger it. It's acceptable to include these tests in the installer workflows to verify the fix.\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm bug: uninstaller not working when the… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest wasmedge-runtime to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "It's on macOS. Additionally, according to the report, it will occur on both Intel and Apple Silicon platforms. The root cause of this issue is that the command usage of `sed` differs between macOS and Linux.",

+    }

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "wasmedge-runtime-3185-bug-uninstaller-not-working-when-the-bash-profile-is-not-a",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "wasmedge-runtime: bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.",
+    "description": "bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.. This issue affects 1+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify wasmedge-runtime troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your wasmedge-runtime deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime -n wasmedge-runtime --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review wasmedge-runtime configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant wasmedge-runtime configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime\nkubectl get configmap -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmedge-runtime\n```\nPlease check this issue:\nhttps://github.com/second-state/LlamaEdge/issues/100\n\nSomeone is running the `installer`. However, it will run the uninstaller first to remove the previous installation. Then, it encountered the bug."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is",
+        "description": "This PR fixes the uninstaller script to handle cases where the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.\n\n### Changes\n- Checks if `.bash_profile` is a symbolic link and informs the user to update it manually.\n- Adjusts the `sed` command for macOS compatibility.\n- Informs the user to update manually if the file is neither regular nor symbolic.\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm bug: uninstaller not working when the is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime -n wasmedge-runtime --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n wasmedge-runtime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This PR fixes the uninstaller script to handle cases where the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.\n\n### Changes\n- Checks if `.bash_profile` is a symbolic link and informs the user to update it manually.\n- Adjusts the `sed` command for macOS compatibility.\n- Informs the user to update manually if the file is neither regular nor symbolic.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+    },
